# williswee.com

> A personal website and portfolio showcasing projects, readings, and guides.

## Main
- [Home](https://williswee.com/index.html): Introduction and overview of the site.
- [Work](https://williswee.com/work.html): A showcase of Willis's professional milestones and projects.
- [Books](https://williswee.com/books.html): A curated list of books I have read and recommend.
- [Guide](https://williswee.com/guide.html): A collection of guides and tutorials on various topics.

## Thoughts
Articles and reflections by Willis Wee.
- [You shouldn't have to lose real money to learn investing](https://williswee.com/thoughts/tickertown): We built TickerTown: an 8-bit game where you can test real stock investing strategies using real market data and fake money.
- [My kids wrote a family success plan. Their user guides are next.](https://williswee.com/thoughts/famplan): We wrote down how our family should work. The kids had opinions. A reflection on running a family huddle, writing a family success plan, and the parallels between parenting and running a startup.
- [Small disclosures build bonds faster than time](https://williswee.com/thoughts/userguide): That is true for colleagues. But it is also true for friends, partners, parents, and kids. A reflection on the power of user guides and small disclosures in building trust faster.
- [When work goes quiet](https://williswee.com/thoughts/nowork): Reflections on balancing work and family as a founder, the system that held both up for 16 years, and what happens when the work variable goes quiet.
- [What I did at TIA](https://williswee.com/thoughts/whatidid): A reflection on my 16 years running Tech in Asia end-to-end, the things we built, the failures, and the hidden heroes.
- [Mission-driven founders](https://williswee.com/thoughts/mission): Reflections on why mission-driven founders are rare and how obsession with a mission aligns teams and acts as a painkiller during hardships.
- [One month into the break](https://williswee.com/thoughts/onemonth): Reflections on life, learning, and building side projects one month after leaving Tech in Asia.
- [Thank you and goodbye, Tech in Asia](https://williswee.com/thoughts/goodbye): After 16 years of building Tech in Asia, Willis reflects on his journey and steps down as CEO.
- [Tech in Asia x SPH Media](https://williswee.com/thoughts/tiasph): Exciting news on SPH Media's plan to acquire Tech in Asia, scaling our mission and preserving our team's independence and culture.
- [A letter to founders who are burning out](https://williswee.com/thoughts/burnout): Reflections on the struggles founders face during an economic downturn, sharing lessons learned from my own journey with Tech in Asia.
- [18 essential reminders from one founder to another](https://williswee.com/thoughts/reminders): From having a clear vision to being kind, here are 18 essential reminders for those ready to take on the challenge of building a startup.
- [Founders are being hit hard by layoffs, too](https://williswee.com/thoughts/hithard): Reflections on the struggles and immense stress founders face during layoffs, and why the growth-at-all-costs model is heavily criticized.
- [Tech in Asia is carbon neutral in time for Earth Day](https://williswee.com/thoughts/carbonneutral): Reflections on carbon accounting, the impact of tech businesses on the environment, and Tech in Asia's journey to becoming carbon neutral.
- [10 lessons from 10 years of Tech in Asia](https://williswee.com/thoughts/ten): Reflecting on 10 lessons learned over the past decade building Tech in Asia.
- [There’s no such thing as a perfect layoff](https://williswee.com/thoughts/layoffsucks): Reflections on why executing layoffs is incredibly difficult, and sharing perspectives from my own experience with a layoff in 2018.
- [The stress (and mindfuck) curve](https://williswee.com/thoughts/stress): Trading notes on dealing with pressure as a founder during the COVID-19 pandemic and navigating the comfort and strain zones.
- [For the first time, we issued bonuses to our team](https://williswee.com/thoughts/profitable): Reflections on reaching profitability in 2019, giving bonuses to the team, and taking control of the company's fate.
- [Keep your head down and just get shit done](https://williswee.com/thoughts/getshitdone): Reflections on Sea Group's success and why successful founders tend to keep their heads down and focus on getting work done.
- [It's been a rough year. Here's how we're bouncing back](https://williswee.com/thoughts/bounce): Reflecting on a challenging 2018 for Tech in Asia, sharing lessons learned and our path to recovery and bouncing back stronger.
- [Don’t delay building your startup culture](https://williswee.com/thoughts/buildculture): Reflecting on how culture has turned out to be one of the key competitive advantages for Tech in Asia.
- [Joining Y Combinator](https://williswee.com/thoughts/yc): Reflections on joining the Y Combinator Winter 2015 batch with Tech in Asia and avoiding complacency.
- [A startup experiment that could snowball into something bigger](https://williswee.com/thoughts/tiaexperiment): Reflections on building the startup ecosystem in Asia and Tech in Asia's role as a pan-Asian startup experiment.
- [The biggest problem facing Southeast Asian startups isn’t the ecosystem](https://williswee.com/thoughts/youaretheproblem): A reflection on the real issue in the Southeast Asian startup ecosystem: the need for better entrepreneurs who focus on building awesome products.
- [Raising a small round forces you to bootstrap](https://williswee.com/thoughts/smallround): A reflection on the benefits of raising a small round of funding and the advantages of bootstrapping for early-stage startups.
- [Learning the hard way](https://williswee.com/thoughts/thehardway): Reflections on how entrepreneurs often learn their most valuable lessons through failure and adversity.
- [Take some risk](https://williswee.com/thoughts/risk): A reflection on the startup ecosystem in Singapore, encouraging entrepreneurs to take more risks and expand abroad.
- [Expanding abroad: Risky, scary, and exciting](https://williswee.com/thoughts/abroad): Reflections on the challenges and excitement of expanding a startup beyond Singapore and across Asia.
- [Fight hard with no regrets](https://williswee.com/thoughts/dontdie): A reminder to myself, my team members, and fellow entrepreneurs to fight hard so you have no regrets.
- [My journey as an entrepreneur (so far)](https://williswee.com/thoughts/journey): Reflecting on my early entrepreneurial journey from childhood grasshopper trading to founding Tech in Asia.
